questions.q: In which city is the City Palace located?
questions.explanation: The City Palace complex lies at the heart of Jaipur, the capital of Rajasthan.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the City Palace, Jaipur situated?
questions.explanation: Jaipur is the capital of the state of Rajasthan in northwestern India.

questions.q: Which ruler began the construction of the City Palace, Jaipur?
questions.explanation: Sawai Jai Singh II, the founder of Jaipur, began building the City Palace in the early 18th century.

questions.q: The City Palace served as the seat of which kind of ruler?
questions.explanation: The palace was the royal residence and seat of the Maharajas of Jaipur.

questions.q: Jaipur is popularly known by which colour-based nickname?
questions.explanation: Jaipur is famously called the 'Pink City' for the pink-hued buildings of its old walled town, where the City Palace stands.

questions.q: What does the City Palace primarily function as today, besides being a royal residence?
questions.explanation: A large part of the City Palace is now a museum open to visitors, while the royal family still occupies part of it.

questions.q: The City Palace complex is located near which other famous Jaipur landmark, an astronomical observatory?
questions.explanation: The Jantar Mantar observatory, also built by Jai Singh II, stands right beside the City Palace.

questions.q: Which family is still associated with living in part of the City Palace?
questions.explanation: The erstwhile royal family of Jaipur continues to reside in a portion of the City Palace, such as the Chandra Mahal.

questions.q: The City Palace is broadly built in a style blending Rajput architecture with which other tradition?
questions.explanation: The complex blends Rajput, Mughal and later European influences in its design.

questions.q: Roughly in which century did construction of the City Palace begin?
questions.explanation: Construction began in the early 18th century, around the time Jai Singh II founded Jaipur in 1727.

questions.q: In which year is Jaipur generally said to have been founded by Sawai Jai Singh II?
questions.explanation: Sawai Jai Singh II founded Jaipur in 1727, shifting his capital from the older hill fort of Amber.

questions.q: The seven-storey palace within the City Palace complex that houses the royal family is called what?
questions.explanation: The Chandra Mahal ('Moon Palace') is the seven-storey building still occupied by the royal family.

questions.q: What is the name of the public audience hall in the City Palace that displays art and manuscripts?
questions.explanation: The Diwan-i-Aam (Hall of Public Audience) now serves as an art gallery within the palace museum.

questions.q: The City Palace's Diwan-i-Khas is famous for holding two of the world's largest objects of what kind?
questions.explanation: The Diwan-i-Khas houses two giant silver urns (gangajalis), among the largest silver vessels in the world.

questions.q: Which maharaja reportedly had the giant silver urns made to carry Ganges water to England?
questions.explanation: Sawai Madho Singh II is said to have had the silver urns made to carry sacred Ganges water for his 1902 voyage to England.

questions.q: Which inner courtyard of the City Palace is famous for its four decorated 'season' gates?
questions.explanation: Pritam Niwas Chowk is the inner courtyard with four ornate gates representing the seasons, such as the famous Peacock Gate.

questions.q: The celebrated Peacock Gate in the City Palace's inner courtyard is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Peacock Gate, with its vivid peacock motifs, is associated with Lord Vishnu among the four seasonal gates.

questions.q: Before founding Jaipur, the rulers had their capital at which nearby fortified town?
questions.explanation: The Kachhwaha rulers moved their capital from Amber (Amer) to the new planned city of Jaipur.

questions.q: Which royal dynasty (clan) did the builders of the City Palace belong to?
questions.explanation: The rulers of Jaipur belonged to the Kachhwaha clan of Rajputs.

questions.q: Sawai Jai Singh II is credited with planning Jaipur according to principles from which ancient Indian treatise?
questions.explanation: Jaipur was laid out on a grid based on Vastu Shastra principles of town planning.

questions.q: An architect often credited with helping plan Jaipur and its palace was a Bengali scholar named what?
questions.explanation: Vidyadhar Bhattacharya is traditionally credited as the chief planner of Jaipur's grid layout and palace.

questions.q: The City Palace's textile and costume museum displays garments belonging to which famously large 18th-century maharaja?
questions.explanation: The museum displays the enormous robes of Sawai Madho Singh I, who was said to be of very large stature.

questions.q: The walled city of Jaipur, which contains the City Palace, was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in which year?
questions.explanation: The historic walled 'Pink City' of Jaipur was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 2019.

questions.q: The honorific 'Sawai' adopted by the Jaipur rulers is said to have been granted by which Mughal emperor to Jai Singh II?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that the title 'Sawai' (one and a quarter) was conferred on Jai Singh II by the Mughal emperor Aurangzeb.

questions.q: The two enormous silver urns of the City Palace hold the record (per Guinness) as the largest objects of what material?
questions.explanation: Each urn weighs around 340 kg and they are recognised as the world's largest sterling silver vessels.

questions.q: The City Palace gate flanked by a Naqqar Khana (drum house) and leading toward the inner palace is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The main approach passes through gateways such as the Sireh Deorhi and Virendra Pol, near the drum house (Naqqar Khana).

questions.q: The art gallery in the City Palace is especially noted for a collection of miniature paintings from which school?
questions.explanation: The palace museum holds a fine collection of Rajput and Mughal miniature paintings and illustrated manuscripts.

questions.q: The Maharaja Sawai Man Singh II Museum, housed in the City Palace, is named after a ruler who later became the first what of Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: Sawai Man Singh II became the Rajpramukh, the constitutional head of the newly formed state of Rajasthan, after 1949.

questions.q: The City Palace armoury (Sileh Khana / Maharani Palace) is famous for displaying a large collection of what?
questions.explanation: The armoury houses one of the finest collections of historic weapons, swords, daggers and armour in India.

questions.q: Sawai Jai Singh II, who began the City Palace, was renowned not only as a ruler but also as a what?
questions.explanation: Jai Singh II was a noted astronomer and mathematician who built the Jantar Mantar observatories alongside his palace.
